title: ctx_ptr takes &mut self — defensible against &self simplification
description: Client::ctx_ptr returns a raw *mut whClientContext and takes &mut self. Taking &mut self is the correct choice: it statically prevents two callers from simultaneously holding raw pointers to the same C context, which would be UB via aliased mutable pointers. A contributor might change this to &self with the reasoning that 'we're just getting a pointer, not modifying Rust state.' That reasoning is wrong — the C context has internal mutable state (socket buffers, sequence numbers, etc.) that is mutated on every call, and &self would allow safe Rust to create two &mut whClientContext aliases by calling ctx_ptr twice under shared access.
